title: alchemilla coriacea var. straminea
intro: Alchemilla coriacea var. straminea, also known as a variety of the alpine lady's mantle, is a plant species in the family Rosaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-arthritic effects, which can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being. Alchemilla coriacea var. straminea can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, and its dried leaves and stems can be used to create herbal teas, tinctures, and ointments.
